untranslatable_reason: Signal requires detection of external events (index composition change announcements - stock additions/deletions) which are not available as market indicators. The specific entry trigger is event-driven (announcement timing) rather than price/technical based. Volume surge is used as a proxy but cannot reliably capture the actual announcement event.
translation_notes: This is an event-driven signal that cannot be fully translated into technical indicator rules. The core logic depends on detecting when a stock is added to or deleted from an index, which requires external data not available in the indicator set. A volume spike has been used as a loose proxy for the announcement/rebalancing event, with intraday holding (1 day max) and tight stops (2%) to capture short-term momentum. Direction is context-dependent: LONG on additions (buying pressure), SHORT on deletions (selling pressure). Manual event detection or alternative data source required for proper implementation.
